label: The Vault Heist
subject: Binary search (code)
bossHook: Sixteen vaults, one holds the loot, and you get four tries. Split the row in half every time and the safe number can't hide.

vocab.hint: Crack a vault to see if the target is higher or lower — then always split what's LEFT in half.

first-cut.title: The First Cut
first-cut.hook: Eight vaults, sorted low to high. Don't start at the end — crack the MIDDLE one first. It tells you which half the target hides in.
first-cut.winText: One cut down the middle, then the middle of what was left. That halving is the whole trick behind binary search.

learn.frame: The row is sorted but you can only crack a few — so which vault do you hit FIRST?

reveal.body: Cracking the MIDDLE vault told you which half the target sits in — so one try wiped out the other half. Always start at the middle: that's the move everything else is built on.
reveal.inTheWild: the 'higher or lower' guessing game — the fastest player always guesses the middle number.

halving.title: Cut It in Half
halving.hook: Twelve vaults now. Each crack should throw away HALF of what's left — not shuffle along one at a time.
halving.winText: You threw away half the vaults with every crack — twelve narrowed to one in a handful of tries. That's logarithmic speed.

failText.not-halving: You found it, but you crept along one vault at a time. Crack the MIDDLE of the remaining range each try and it collapses in half every step.

reveal.body: Every crack landed on the middle of the LIVE range, so twelve became six became three. Cutting what's left in half each try is what makes the search fast.
reveal.inTheWild: log₂(n) tries: a thousand vaults cracked in ten, a million in twenty.

insights.not-halving: Creeping vault by vault still finds the loot — but that's a linear sweep, n tries. Halving is the whole point: each cut discards half the row, turning n into log n. Give that up and sorted vaults bought you nothing.

trust-clues.title: Trust the Clues
trust-clues.hook: After a crack, a 'higher/lower' clue rules out a whole side of the row (it dims). Never re-check a ruled-out vault.
trust-clues.winText: You believed the clues and never re-opened a dimmed vault. Every probe should live inside the range the feedback leaves alive.

failText.ignored-feedback: You cracked a vault the clues had already ruled out — a wasted try. Once a side is dimmed, the target CANNOT be there. Stay inside the live range.

reveal.body: Each 'higher/lower' clue killed an entire side of the row, and you only ever probed what was still lit. Using the clue to throw away half is what keeps the range shrinking.
reveal.inTheWild: git bisect, a database index seek — each comparison says which side to keep and which to drop.

insights.ignored-feedback: The clue isn't a hint, it's a guarantee: sorted order proves the target is on one exact side. Re-cracking a dimmed vault re-searches space you already ruled out — the feedback is the only thing that makes halving legal.

boss-vault.title: BOSS · The Grand Vault
boss-vault.hook: Sixteen vaults. Four tries. No margin. Middle, then middle, then middle — perfect binary search or nothing.
boss-vault.winText: Sixteen down to one in four clean cuts. That's a flawless binary search — heist complete.

failText.not-halving: Too many tries — you didn't halve hard enough. Sixteen vaults needs only four perfect cuts.
failText.ignored-feedback: You opened a ruled-out vault under pressure. Trust every clue — it's what makes four tries enough.

learn.frame: Both halves now, no hints on-screen: split the row in half AND obey every clue — what does that add up to?

reveal.body: Middle to split, the clue to pick the surviving side, repeat — sixteen vaults collapse to one in four cuts. Halving driven by the feedback, all the way down, IS binary search.
reveal.inTheWild: std::lower_bound, a DB index seek, git bisect — every one is halve-then-follow-the-comparison.

insights.not-halving: The halving is the speed: four cracks only reach sixteen vaults if each one throws away half the row. Slide instead of cut and four tries can't cover it.
insights.ignored-feedback: The feedback is what makes each cut valid — it proves which half to drop. Ignore it and you're halving blind, with no guarantee the loot is even in the side you kept.

narrow-drill.title: SIDE-QUEST · Narrow It Down
narrow-drill.hook: Let's drill ONE reflex until it's automatic: after every crack, jump to the MIDDLE of whatever's still lit. Eight vaults, go.
narrow-drill.winText: Middle, middle, middle — you cut the range in half every single time. That reflex IS binary search.

reveal.body: After each crack you jumped straight to the middle of the lit range — nothing fancier. Middle, middle, middle until one vault is left is the engine underneath it all.
reveal.inTheWild: the one line at the heart of it: mid = lo + (hi − lo) / 2, every iteration.
